'''Luke Thompson''' (born 27 April 1995) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and for [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.

He has played his most of his professional career to date for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], with whom he won the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]]. He has also spent time on loan from Saints at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[2015 Championship 1|Championship 1]].

==Background==
Thompson was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester, England]].

== Career ==
===St Helens===
Thompson had been at [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] since he was 11 (signing from Bold Miners).{{citation needed|date=April 2022}}

==== 2013 ====
Thompson made his [[Super League]] debut during the 2013 season in the Saints' 21-14 round 9 loss against the [[London Broncos]]. On 27 September, Thompson's contract was extended by 2 years.<ref>{{Cite web |last=test |date=2013-09-27 |title=Saints looking to the future |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/saints-looking-to-the-future/ |access-date=2023-02-28 |website=LoveRugbyLeague |language=en-GB}}</ref> Thompson played 7 games for St Helens and scored 3 tries.

==== 2014 ====
In Round 21 of the [[Super League XIX|2014 Super League season]], Thompson scored a try in the Saints' 58-16 win over the London Broncos.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Super League XIX 2014 - Week 21 - London Broncos 16 lost to St Helens 58 |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xix-2014/round-21/london-vs-st-helens/summary.html |access-date=2023-02-28 |website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref> St Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] and Thompson was selected to play from the interchange bench in their 14-6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|access-date=12 October 2014|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref> Thompson played 17 games for St Helens and scored a try.

==== 2015 ====
[[File:Luke Thompson.jpg|thumb|150px|right|Thompson playing for St Helens in 2015]]
Thompson played 20 games for St Helens during the [[Super League XX|2015 Super League season]], including their Super 8s campaign.

==== 2016 ====
Thompson continued playing with St Helens during the [[Super League XXI|2016 Super League season]], including their 10-18 semi-final loss to [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Super League XXI 2016 - Semi Final - Warrington Wolves 18 def. St Helens 10 |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xxi-2016/semi-final/warrington-vs-st-helens/summary.html |access-date=2023-02-28 |website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref> He finished the year playing 23 games and scoring 2 tries.

==== 2017 ====
Thompson played in St Helens' 22-23 semi-final loss to [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Super League XXII 2017 - Semi Final - Castleford Tigers 23 def. St Helens 22 |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xxii-2017/semi-final/castleford-vs-st-helens/summary.html |access-date=2023-02-28 |website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref> He finished the year with 27 games and 2 tries.

==== 2018 ====
In 2018, Thompson had one of his best years yet, playing 32 games and scoring 6 tries. He played in St Helens' 13-18 loss to Warrington.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Super League XXIII 2018 - Semi Final - St Helens 13 lost to Warrington Wolves 18 |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xxiii-2018/semi-final/st-helens-vs-warrington/summary.html |access-date=2023-02-28 |website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>

==== 2019 ====
He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat to the [[Warrington Wolves]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>
He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Salford Red Devils]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===Canterbury-Bankstown===
==== 2020 ====
On 4 February, it was announced Thompson had signed a three-year deal with [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-02-03 |title=Bulldogs sign star English International forward Luke Thompson for three years |url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2020/02/04/bulldogs-sign-star-english-international-forward-luke-thompson-for-three-years/ |access-date=2023-02-28 |website=Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs |language=en}}</ref> Thompson made the move to Australia in June. He completed two weeks of isolation on his arrival in Australia before his first game for the Canterbury-Bankstown club.<ref>{{Citation |title=Luke Thompson training in isolation |url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2020/06/18/luke-thompson-training-in-isolation/ |language=en |access-date=2023-02-28}}</ref>

Thompson made his debut for the Bulldogs against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in round 9 of the [[2020 NRL season]].{{citation needed|date=April 2022}}
In round 20 of the [[2020 NRL season]], Thompson was placed on report for reportedly eye gouging one of the [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] players during Canterbury's 42-0 loss.  Thompson was later suspended for four matches after being found guilty.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/09/29/thompson-cops-four-week-ban-for-eye-gouge/?_gl=1*17xipt5*_ga*clJLNnpLd3d0cHFndjZldENaMWFtVEFsRzNnRFJNVXpGbDhhaWduSGNvTG92OWJnMU5XSHZOeEloTTFlT3pVRw../|title=Thompson cops four-week ban for eye gouge; King three for hip drop|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>
Thompson made 10 appearances for Canterbury in the [[2020 NRL season]] as the club narrowly avoided the wooden spoon finishing 15th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/nrl-2020-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-announce-eight-player-departures-kieran-foran-aiden-tolman-full-list/news-story/67fbadc55112e80ee342e26861bd206d|title=Dogs confirm EIGHT-man clean-out in first glimpse of Barrett era|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=22 September 2020}}</ref>

==== 2021 ====
In round 5 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Thompson scored his first try for Canterbury in a 52-18 loss against [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]]. This try ended Canterbury's scoreless streak of over 240 minutes of play, as they had failed to score in their previous three games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/04/10/storm-pack-too-much-power-for-bulldogs-in-nine-try-blitz/|title=Bellamy wants more from Storm after Bulldogs romp|website=www.nrl.com|date=10 April 2021}}</ref>

In round 19 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Thompson scored another try but was subsequently sent to the sin bin during Canterbury's 44-24 loss to [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]].   As a result of the sin binning incident, Thompson received a three-match ban on 27 July 2021.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-07-25/nrl-live-dragons-titans-bulldogs-sharks-score-updates/100308698/|title=Cronulla Sharks beat Canterbury Bulldogs 44-24, Gold Coast Titans down St George Illawarra Dragons 32-10|newspaper=ABC News|date=25 July 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-nrl-judiciary-luke-thompson-hit-patrick-herbert-gold-coast-titans/news-story/15db0c5cb9de8e136b6a14c06d266f40|title=Luke Thompson hit with ban|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=27 July 2021}}</ref>

Throughout the [[2021 NRL season]] Thompson made a total of 15 appearances for Canterbury. However, the club finished last in the season and were awarded the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/canterbury-bulldogs-set-to-sign-max-king-and-braidon-burns/a9fa54c9-a224-4cc9-ae15-8a5dd1e74275/|title=Canterbury Bulldogs set to sign Rabbitohs star Braidon Burns, Storm prop Max King for 2022 NRL season|website=wwos.nine.com.au}}</ref>

==== 2022 ====
Thompson played a total of 13 matches for Canterbury in the [[2022 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review/wapmyddrbadukioadiybr1fs/|title=NRL 2022: Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

==== 2023 ====
On the 28 February, Thompson injured his ankle at training, and was assisted off the field. Canterbury general manager Phil Gould later revealed that the injury was quite serious, and initial prognosis was minimum 6 months recovery.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Grim update on $800k star after 'serious' injury |url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/news-2023-luke-thompson-canterbury-bulldogs-injury-phil-gould/d35755be-5206-4077-8edf-be689ca8af6a |access-date=2023-02-28 |website=wwos.nine.com.au |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite tweet|number=1630458714393313280|user=PhilGould15|title=Unfortunately Luke Thompson’s injury is quite serious. Prognosis is for minimum 6 months recovery. Possibly longer depending on surgery type. Very sad for Luke and the club. Thunderbolts happen.|first=Phil|last=Gould|date=2023-02-28|access-date=2023-02-28}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-casualty-ward-injuries-outs-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-luke-thompson-out-for-season-phil-gould-news-team-lists/news-story/fa65f07f663ceec7cc157b9d5ba5a63b/|title='Quite serious': Bulldogs' 'very sad' injury blow on eve of NRL season — Casualty Ward|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref> Thompson made his return on 28 July off the bench in Round 22 of the [[NSW Cup]] season, making 10 runs for 103 metres.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Bulldogs v Roosters |url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/draw/nsw-cup/2023/round-22/bulldogs-v-roosters/ |access-date=2023-07-29 |website=New South Wales Rugby League |language=en}}</ref>
On 4 October, Thompson signed a four-year deal to join English side [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] ahead of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67011708|title=Luke Thompson: Wigan Warriors sign England and ex-St Helens prop on four-year deal|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Wigan===
====2024====
On 8 June, Thompson played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October, Thompson played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

====2025====
On 9 October, Thompson played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===2026===
On 30 May, Thompson played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship/title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2018 he was selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].<ref name="Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|publisher=Guardian|date=17 October 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref>

He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.<ref name="Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea">{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|publisher=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref> He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat to Tonga.<ref name="Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|publisher=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}}</ref>
